title: Defining a Structure
ques: Define a structure named **`Student`** with three members: `name` (string), `id` (int), and `marks` (float). Create an instance and print its size.
explanation: A structure groups variables of different types. `sizeof(s1)` returns the total memory allocated for the structure (including any padding).

title: Accessing Members
ques: Declare a `point` structure with `x` and `y` coordinates. Initialize it to `(10, 20)` and print the coordinates using the **dot operator** (`.`).
explanation: The dot operator `.` is used to access members of a structure variable.

title: Array of Structures
ques: Create an array of **3 Book structures** (members: `title`, `price`). Initialize them and loop through to print the details.
explanation: We can create arrays where each element is a structure. `library[i]` accesses the specific structure, and `.title` accesses the member.

title: Pointer to Structure
ques: Declare a pointer to a `Person` structure (members: `age`). Assign it the address of a variable and access `age` using the **arrow operator** (`->`).
explanation: When accessing structure members via a pointer, use the arrow operator `->`. `ptr->age` is equivalent to `(*ptr).age`.

title: Typedef with Structs
ques: Use `typedef` to create an alias **`s_emp`** for `struct Employee` and use it to declare a variable.
explanation: `typedef` simplifies syntax, allowing us to use `s_emp` instead of writing `struct Employee` every time.

title: Nested Structures
ques: Create a structure `Date` (day, month, year) and nest it inside a `Student` structure. Initialize and print the student's **birth year**.
explanation: Structures can contain other structures. We access inner members using chained dot operators: `s.dob.year`.

title: Unions
ques: Define a **Union** named `Data` with an `int` and a `float`. Assign proper values and explain why only one member holds a valid value at a time.
explanation: Unions share the same memory location for all members. Writing to one member overwrites the others. Size of union = size of largest member.

title: Passing Struct to Function
ques: Write a function `printPoint` that accepts a `Point` structure (by value) and prints its coordinates.
explanation: Structures can be passed to functions just like primitive types. However, for large structures, passing by pointer is more efficient.

title: Bit Fields
ques: Define a structure with **bit fields** to store a `flag` (1 bit) and a `status` (4 bits). Print the size of the structure.
explanation: Bit fields allow concise storage by specifying the exact number of bits a member requires. useful for hardware register access or compressing flags.
